questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 50° and angle B measures 60°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So angle C = 180° − 50° − 60° = 70°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one acute angle measuring 35°. What is the measure of the other acute angle?
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the two acute angles sum to 90°. So the other angle = 90° − 35° = 55°.

questions.question: In an equilateral triangle, what is the measure of each interior angle?
questions.explanation: All three angles in an equilateral triangle are equal. Since they sum to 180°, each angle = 180° ÷ 3 = 60°.

questions.question: Two angles in a triangle measure 45° and 45°. Is this triangle a right triangle? Explain briefly.
questions.explanation: The third angle = 180° − 45° − 45° = 90°. A triangle with a 90° angle is a right triangle.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, angle P = 28° and angle Q = 90°. Find angle R.
An isosceles triangle has a vertex angle of 80°. What is the measure of each base angle?
questions.explanation: The two base angles in an isosceles triangle are equal. If each is x, then x + x + 80° = 180°, so 2x = 100°, and x = 50°.
